title: NEET UG leak: CBI conducts searches at Renukai Chemistry Classes in Latur
content: CBI officials began the search at Renukai Chemistry Classes in the city’s Shivnagar locality in the afternoon, and it was still underway till late evening

title: With CM and three Ministers, Ernakulam gets strong representation in Cabinet
content: Angamaly MLA Roji M. John of the Congress, Piravom legislator Anoop Jacob of Kerala Congress (Jacob), and Kalamassery MLA V. E. Abdul Gafoor of the Indian Union Muslim League will be sworn in as Ministers

title: India, Sweden agree to elevate bilateral ties to Strategic Partnership | LIVE
content: Sweden confers Royal Order of Polar Star upon PM Modi; it is the 31st global honour for Prime Minister Narendra Modi

title: E-buses will not lead to privatisation of RTC, says top official
content: The government is commitment to providing safe, affordable, efficient and environment-friendly transport services to the people through APSRTC and fully protecting the interests of its employees, says Krishna Babu, Special Chief Secretary, Transport

title: RTC employees’ union opposes privatisation move
content: Accusing the government of trying to hand over valuable assets and operations to private companies in the name of electric bus services, it calls for a round-table with various trade unions in Vijayawada on May 26 to decide the future course of action

title: New Bengaluru–Mumbai Express flagged off; Vande Bharat sleeper service soon
content: Railway Minister says around 1,750 km of new railway lines have been constructed in Karnataka since 2014

title: Vijayawada GGH officials probing why biomedical waste room was kept unlocked
content: There have been internal disputes within the hospital and some outsourcing staff might have conspired in the incident, allege GGH staff

title: Anagha from Tirthahalli emerges State topper in SSLC after revaluation
content: Anagha, daughter of Shivakumar and Mangala, both teachers, had scored 100% in every subject except Social Science, where she received 99

title: Mysuru: Jackfruit festival concludes with a call to conserve indigenous varieties
content: The organisers said the two-day jackfruit festival successfully attracted large crowds, with more than 6,000 visitors attending the fair.

title: I did not try to broker a deal between DMK and AIADMK: Rajinikanth
content: Rajinikanth asked how he could harbour envy against Mr. Vijay when there is a big generation gap between them

title: Decision on letters submitted by both groups of AIADMK MLAs will be taken at appropriate time: J.C.D. Prabhakar
content: The Speaker says no specific time limit has been stipulated in Assembly Rules or law for taking a decision on letters submitted to the Speaker. He inspects playgrounds in his Thousand Lights constituency

title: Trade, energy, discussions on global conflicts to top agenda for Modi’s Nordic visit
content: First Indian Prime Minister’s bilateral visit in 43 years, first Nordic summit since 2022 to kick off this week in Oslo.

title: Woman stripped, paraded with slipper garland after temple entry dispute; two women arrested
content: Assault took place after a youth from the victim’s family defied a caste panchayat’s ban on the family entering the local temple; videos show bystanders watching and recording on mobile phones

title: Congress leader slams Andhra Pradesh CM’s population growth incentive
content: Despite southern States’ fear of losing political representation due to their declining population, incentivising families to have more babies is the wrong approach, says former RS MP Husain Dalwai

title: Throwing non-veg food waste in Ganga could hurt religious sentiments: Allahabad HC on iftar party row
content: The FIR was lodged on March 16 based on a complaint filed by Rajat Jaiswal, the president of the Varanasi chapter of the BJP Yuva Morcha, claiming that the incident hurt the sentiments of the Hindus.

title: High-level inquiry committee set up to probe fire in Rajdhani Express: Railway Board
content: The fire erupted at 5.15 a.m. in the B-1 passenger coach of the train between Vikramgarh Alot and Lunirichha stations under the Kota Railway division, one-and-a-half hours after it left the Ratlam Junction in Madhya Pradesh

title: Parth Pawar meets Fadnavis amid reports of rift within NCP
content: NCP sources said the two leaders briefly discussed issues within the NCP as well. “A seat in Rajya Sabha is getting vacant. We have a few names in mind. This was also discussed,” a leader said.

title: Amaravati Amma Vanta empowering women through traditional food ventures
content: The initiative has helped create sustainable self-employment opportunities, with each entrepreneur now earning an average monthly income of around ₹12,000, says CRDA Group Director (Social Development)

title: Clashes erupt in West Bengal over prayer restrictions
content: Congress leader Adhir Ranjan Chowdhury has sought the Chief Minister’s intervention to mitigate the tensions triggered by the government’s recent guidelines on animal slaughter

title: Kerala Cabinet Formation updates: UDF finalises Kerala Cabinet list, IUML gets five ministerial posts
content: Senior Congress leader Thiruvanchoor Radhakrishnan will be Speaker of Kerala Assembly; 21-member Cabinet with CM-designate Satheesan will take oath of office on May 18

title: Ladakh administration hikes wages of skilled, unskilled workers
content: Lieutenant-Governor announced a major wage rationalisation reform for daily wagers, part-time, seasonal and need-based employees working in the Union Territory

title: Mehbooba wants Pakistan to respond ‘positively’ after Hosabale calls for talks
content: Underlining that the “solution to Kashmir lies in dialogue and within the framework of the Constitution”, Ms. Mufti said, “We believe in engagement and talks. We seek peace with dignity.”

title: Great Nicobar project a recipe for ecological disaster: Jairam Ramesh
content: The Central government has been citing strategic interests for going ahead, for which the Congress leader has suggested alternative means in a letter the Defence Minister

title: Trinamool leaders face arrests, public anger, return extorted money
content: The crackdown intensified after Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari urged people to file complaints against those who had allegedly extorted money from them during the previous Trinamool Congress regime

title: Education Ministry a department of disasters, sack Pradhan: Rahul Gandhi
content: ‘Every announcement plunges children deeper into uncertainty. Every failure goes unpunished,’ the Congress leader said

title: Satheesan names 20 Ministers; Kerala Cabinet to take oath on May 18
content: Of the 63 Congress MLAs in the 102-strong UDF contingent, many eligible names could not be given Ministerial berths as the party had to consider social and regional equations, V.D. Satheesan said

title: Centre defends on-screen marking for CBSE Class 12 answer sheets, reduces re-evaluation fee
content: Union Secretary of School Education Sanjay Kumar and CBSE Chairman Rahul Singh say the system will improve evaluation and transparency. Re-evaluation cost has been reduced to ₹100 per answer sheet

title: Suspicion clouds death of pregnant woman in car blaze in Kozhikode
content: Police officers from Meppayur station confirm that all aspects of the case, including forensic evidence, witness statements and family allegations, will be examined in detail as part of the ongoing investigation into the incident

title: Galaxy CC win The Hindu FIC–AM/NS India Cricket Trophy
content: Lakshman’s unbeaten 94 and Ashish’s six-wicket haul seal a 53-run victory for Galaxy over Cavaliers CC in the final

title: Manipur families seek release of Kuki, Naga captives
content: While Kuki-Zo organisations say 14 community members continue to be held hostage, Naga organisations say six of theirs are yet to be released

title: Boost for tech, trade & defence as India, Sweden become ‘strategic partners’
content: India and Sweden have elevated their ties to a strategic partnership, signing an action plan for 2026-2030 covering green transition, technology, trade, and defence. Prime Minister Modi emphasized shared democratic values and innovation, highlighting growing defence cooperation moving beyond a buyer-seller dynamic. Discussions also explored strengthening supply chains and sustainable mobility.

title: IPL: Riyan Parag’s late gamble backfires as Capitals stay alive with win over RR
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashed his way to a new IPL record for sixes by an Indian batter. Despite his explosive start, Rajasthan Royals could not reach a commanding total. Delhi Capitals chased well initially but also lost wickets. In a tense finish, Delhi Capitals secured a home win, breaking their losing streak.

title: From July 10, US can deny your visa for this one reason
content: USCIS now has the power to reject or deny immigration applications, even keeping fees, if an invalid signature is found months or years later. This new rule, effective July 10, 2026, applies broadly to most benefit requests, including visas and Green Cards. Applicants cannot usually fix signature errors after submission.

title: Ex-Google CEO Eric Schmidt has message for engineers who are still coding the old way
content: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt declared the era of manual code writing is over, citing AI's explosive power. He believes programmers still using traditional methods are behind, as AI enables individuals to build powerful applications previously requiring large teams. This shift is causing a sell-off in software stocks, with companies needing to adapt quickly to survive.

title: Weather & then war lead to tears in India’s onion basket
content: Maharashtra's onion farmers face devastating losses as prices plummet far below production costs. A combination of bumper harvests, weak demand, export disruptions, and unseasonal rains has led to crops rotting and farmers selling at a significant deficit. Many are demanding government intervention and compensation for their distress.

title: Kharif output dips by over 10% across states in El Niño years: Study
content: El Niño's shadow looms over India's monsoon. Past El Niño years saw key kharif crops like paddy and maize suffer over 10% output reduction in numerous districts. This study highlights vulnerable areas and stresses the need for district-level climate-resilient planning. Policymakers are urged to strengthen contingency plans for drought-tolerant varieties and efficient water management to protect farmers.

title: America's largest bank JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon has a three word career advice for everyone
content: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon, at the 2025 Reagan National Economic Forum, urged professionals to "talk to everybody" to foster growth and learning. He emphasized stepping out of comfort zones, observing, and listening to diverse perspectives. Dimon also warned companies against bureaucracy, calling it a "silent killer" that breeds complacency and inefficiency, especially in large organizations.

title: 'Silence of virgin girl can be treated as consent': Taliban formally recognises child marriage
content: The Taliban regime in Afghanistan has enacted a new family law regulation, "Principles of Separation Between Spouses," which has drawn international criticism. A particularly controversial article interprets a virgin girl's silence after puberty as consent to marriage. The decree also addresses child marriage, divorce, and other marital disputes, granting fathers and grandfathers significant authority.

title: Roger Federer and Mirka Federer combined net worth 2026
content: By 2026, Roger Federer and Mirka Federer’s combined net worth will be one of the largest financial empires among athletes in sports history. The wealth has been built up by ATP prize money, record-breaking endorsement deals and strategic investments like On Running, showing the enduring brand power of tennis. Lifestyle management, family operations, and business direction are also big players in the game, along with Roger Federer and Mirka Federer.

title: Andre Agassi and Steffi Graf combined net worth 2026
content: Steffi Graf and Andre Agassi are a very recognizable tennis pair, and they have an estimated net worth in 2026 of $290 million. They’ve become wealthy by winning Grand Slams, signing multiyear contracts and other investments. Both benefited from the brand equity, business pursuits and financial planning that they developed during and after their successful professional tennis careers.

title: FIFA 2026 World Cup row erupts as fans demand BTS removal from Jungkook song
content: Fans are criticizing BigHit Music after BTS was reportedly credited on Jungkook’s solo FIFA World Cup song Dreamers. Many argued that Jungkook alone created and performed the track, calling the move unfair to his solo achievements. The backlash grew amid fresh buzz about BTS potentially performing at the 2026 FIFA World Cup halftime show, reigniting conversations about solo recognition, group branding, and BigHit’s marketing strategy surrounding Jungkook’s global success.

title: Police turn heat on Union minister Bandi Sanjay’s son, add ‘repeated rape’ charge
content: Cyberabad Police have added a serious charge of repeated rape under the new Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita against Bandi Sai Bageerath, son of Union minister Bandi Sanjay, in a Pocso case. He was remanded to judicial custody until May 29. Other charges related to outraging modesty and sexual harassment remain. Bageerath surrendered to police after being on the run.

title: Fire in Rajdhani Express: High-level probe ordered, railway traffic disrupted — what we know so far
content: A fire erupted in the Thiruvananthapuram-Hazrat Nizamuddin Rajdhani Express in Ratlam, Madhya Pradesh. No injuries were reported; all 68 passengers were safely evacuated. The blaze disrupted rail traffic on the busy Mumbai-Delhi route. In a separate incident, a van carrying repair equipment overturned, injuring five railway staff members. Restoration efforts are underway.

title: Accidental bonanza: BMTC staffers get full 26-month arrears, then recall notice arrives
content: A technical error at BMTC's Yeshwanthpur depot-26 led to approximately 100 employees mistakenly receiving 26 months of salary revision arrears instead of the approved 11 months. The corporation quickly rectified the situation, contacting affected staff to return the excess payment through official channels, including a QR scanner at a temple bus stop office.

title: In covert op, Gujarati techie held in US for trying to exploit minors
content: A Gujarati-origin tech professional, Siddharth Sunil Kumar Patel, was arrested in Florida for allegedly attempting to exploit minors. Undercover operations by ICAC led to his apprehension after he engaged in explicit online conversations and traveled for a planned sexual encounter. Patel faces serious felony charges, highlighting ongoing efforts to protect children online.

title: KL Rahul scripts history, breaks AB de Villiers record for most...
content: KL Rahul achieved a significant milestone, becoming the player with the most 50+ scores against Rajasthan Royals in IPL history with his ninth half-century. This also marked his 50th IPL fifty overall, placing him among elite Indian batters. Despite his composed 56, his dismissal at a crucial juncture impacted Delhi's chase.

title: 'Welcome you with ... ': Bengal BJP chief's sarcastic invite to Abhishek for Falta repoll
content: West Bengal BJP chief Samik Bhattacharya sarcastically invited TMC leader Abhishek Banerjee to campaign in Falta ahead of the May 21 repoll, claiming the TMC has been 'sent into exile' by the state's people. Bhattacharya alleged the TMC leadership has disappeared following electoral setbacks and expressed confidence in BJP's victory, urging TMC workers to campaign freely.

title: Salman Khan's cryptic note about loneliness gets fans worried
content: Salman Khan's recent shirtless Instagram post, showcasing his sculpted physique, has sparked widespread attention. While fans praised his fitness, an accompanying emotional caption about being "alone and lonely" has led to speculation about hidden meanings and a desire for his raw acting talent to be seen on screen again.

title: Horoscope Today: Daily astrological predictions for May 18, 2026
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for May 18, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi scripts history, becomes Indian with most...
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, at just 15, has become the Indian batter with the most sixes in a single IPL season, surpassing Abhishek Sharma's record with 43 maximums. His explosive start against Delhi Capitals, hitting a six off the first ball, marked his fifth such instance this season. Sooryavanshi's remarkable six-hitting prowess is rewriting IPL history.

title: Growing up unheard: The truth behind teenage ‘attitude’ and ‘mood swings’
content: Teenagers' emotions are often dismissed as drama or phases, but they can signify real stress, loneliness, or anxiety.  A significant gap exists between how teens and adults communicate and experience pressure, especially with the rise of social media and intense academic competition.  Understanding and listening without judgment are crucial for bridging this disconnect.

title: Indian among 4 dead in Ukrainian drone strike in Russia; Zelenskyy calls attack 'justified'
content: A Ukrainian drone strike in the Moscow region resulted in the death of one Indian worker and injuries to three others. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy stated the attack was justified retaliation for Russia's continued assaults on Ukraine. Russia reported intercepting hundreds of drones in one of the largest offensives of the war.

title: New Keralam govt: VD Satheesan announces cabinet before oath - check full list
content: Kerala chief minister-designate V. D. Satheesan has announced his new cabinet, set to be sworn in on Monday. Prominent leaders like Ramesh Chennithala and Sunny Joseph will be part of the ministry. Thiruvanchoor Radhakrishnan has been named Speaker, with Shanimol Usman as Deputy Speaker.

title: India among fastest-growing steel market as global prices rise: Goldman Sachs
content: Global steel prices surged in April and early May, with India emerging as a fast-growing market. Hot rolled coil prices rose across major regions, led by Brazil. India's crude steel production saw an 11% year-on-year increase in March, outpacing global trends. China's steel output contracted, though capacity cuts are expected to be delayed.

title: ‘Tomorrow again I’m going to see the sunrise’: Shreyas turns philosophical after PBKS loss
content: Despite Punjab Kings' sixth consecutive defeat, captain Shreyas Iyer remains philosophical, emphasizing belief and attitude over results. He acknowledged early batting damage as the turning point in their loss to RCB, stating the match was lost in the powerplay. Iyer expressed disappointment but praised middle-order resistance, looking forward with optimism.

title: 'Leave the noble idli out': Tharoor's witty response to rasgulla comparison goes viral
content: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor humorously defended the South Indian dish idli after a viral post compared it to rasgulla dipped in sugar syrup. Tharoor explained the distinct culinary and biological differences, calling idli a "culinary engineering marvel" and a "savoury monolith of South Indian culinary genius."

title: IPL 2026: Ruthless RCB storm into playoffs as PBKS slump to sixth straight defeat
content: Defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru secured a playoff spot with a dominant 23-run victory over Punjab Kings in Dharamsala. Virat Kohli's energetic performance, coupled with crucial knocks from Devdutt Padikkal and Venkatesh Iyer, propelled RCB to a commanding total. Bhuvneshwar Kumar's early wickets stifled Punjab's chase, highlighting RCB's championship mettle.

title: After Kerala, Karnataka becomes Congress’s next leadership challenge
content: The Kerala Congress leadership change has fueled speculation in Karnataka, with Deputy CM D K Shivakumar's supporters pushing for his elevation to the top post. While the party high command has publicly backed Chief Minister Siddaramaiah, Shivakumar's camp sees the Kerala development as a hopeful precedent. The approaching three-year mark of the government's term intensifies the debate over leadership transition.

title: 'Shimla Agreement Table Stays As Reminder Of Mistakes': Himachal Governor
content: "We had split Pakistan into two. When 90,000 Pakistani soldiers surrendered before you, any demand could have been met. Mistakes were indeed made," Himachal Pradesh Governor Kavinder Gupta said

title: US Iran War News Highlights: Trump's "Clock Ticking" Threat To Iran, Tehran Readies Hormuz Traffic "Mechanism"
content: US Iran War News LIVE Updates: Israel launched a massive series of airstrikes on southern Lebanon, despite an extension of the truce between the two countries.

title: Trump Likely To Hold Key Meeting Over Military Action On Iran: Report
content: The meeting will come after Donald Trump issued a stark warning to Iran on Sunday, saying that the clock in ticking for Tehran.

title: India, Netherlands Sign Pact To Fast Track Freshwater Reservoir In Gujarat
content: To formalise this technical exchange, a Letter of Intent was signed between India's Ministry of Jal Shakti and the Netherlands' Ministry of Infrastructure and Water Management.

title: UP Cabinet Portfolios Allocated Week After Expansion: Who Got What
content: In the second expansion under Yogi Adityanath 2.0, two cabinet ministers and four state ministers were inducted into the Uttar Pradesh government on May 10.

title: 'We're Living In Horror': Family Of Noida Woman Found Dead In Bhopal To NDTV
content: Twisha Sharma's father Navnidhi Sharma and cousin brother Ashish Sharma told NDTV that they have been struggling for justice and running from pillar to post in Bhopal

title: Amit Shah Calls Gujarat Local Body Poll Win "Historic", Slams Rahul Gandhi
content: Speaking at an event in Gandhinagar, Shah launched an attack on the Congress and Aam Aadmi Party over their loss in the polls.

title: Paris Probes Child Abuse By Staff At 84 Kindergartens, 20 Primary Schools
content: Parents in the city have in recent months accused supervisors in charge of children outside the classroom -- such as during recess or before pick-up -- of mistreating, or physically or sexually abusing pupils in their care.

title: PM Modi Receives Sweden's Top Honour, Royal Order Of Polar Star
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi was on Sunday conferred with Sweden's Royal Order of Polar Star, the highest honour awarded to a Head of Government by the Scandinavian nation.

title: Iran Has Found Another Achilles' Heel Lurking Beneath Strait Of Hormuz
content: Tehran is seeking to levy fees on firms including Google, Microsoft, Meta and Amazon for use of the submarine cables that pass beneath the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Video: Rhino Attacks, Tries To Flip Safari Jeep At Assam National Park
content: The incident occurred during a routine jungle safari when the rhino suddenly approached a stationary open jeep carrying tourists.

title: Delhi Government Unveils Rs 10 Crore Loan Plan For Women-Led Startups
content: The Delhi government will offer collateral-free loans of up to Rs 10 crore to women-led startups and self-help groups (SHGs), with the government acting as guarantor, Chief Minister Rekha Gupta announced on Sunday.

title: 20 Ministers In VD Satheesan Cabinet In Kerala, 14 Will Be New Faces
content: In keeping with the Centre's suggestion of responsible fuel usage, Satheesan has ordered that there be no convoy, security vehicles or ambulances. He has also said he would not require a new vehicle.

title: J&K Police Raze 'Narco Palaces' Used As Heroin Trade Hub In Samba
content: The Jammu and Kashmir Police on Sunday demolished 33 illegal structures used as a hub for peddling drugs in the Samba district.

title: Indian Among 3 Killed In Ukrainian Drone Attack On Moscow
content: Embassy officials have visited the site of the attack and met the injured workers in the hospital, the statement added.

title: Ground Report: Delhi's Narela Gets Rs 55-Crore Drainage Upgrade Ahead Of Monsoon
content: The 9.5-kilometre drainage network, being developed under the government's Drainage Master Plan, was inaugurated by Delhi Public Works Department Minister Pravesh Sahib Singh.

title: UAE Nuclear Power Plant Hit By Drone, Triggers Massive Fire
content: The drone hit an electrical generator outside the inner perimeter of the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant, the Abu Dhabi Media Office said.

title: No Actual Concessions In US Response To Iran's Proposal To End War: Report
content: The report added that the US had conditioned the cessation of hostilities on all fronts on the start of negotiations.

title: No laughing matter: Colbert exit sparks fears for political satire in America
content: late-night television is bleeding money in the streaming age, with younger audiences migrating to social media memes, clips, and podcasts hosted by hosts broadcasting from basements and bunkers . the network insists the decision is “purely financial,” which may be true in the same way the Titanic had a “water management issue”

title: PM Modi’s visit to Netherlands secures 17 strategic pacts
content: the visit to the Netherlands ended with 17 outcomes in all, including agreements on migration and mobility, green hydrogen and defence cooperation . modi and Dutch PM Rob Jetten also discussed the situation in west Asia, calling for freedom of navigation and global flow of commerce through Strait of Hormuz .

title: ‘There won’t be anything left’: Trump says ‘clock is ticking’ for Iran as nuclear talks stall

title: PIO gets 14-year jail for abusing 61 women in Australia
content: australian-origin massage therapist sumit Rastogi sentenced to 13 years and 10 months in jail for sexually abusing 61 women over nine months . the 39-year-old was convicted of 97 charges of “aggravated indecent assault” and “secretly filming” the survivors . he said he showed no remorse .

title: Measles kills 450 kids in Bangladesh; probe sought into Yunus’ vax policy
content: a court order has been sought for barring former interim govt chief adviser Muhammad Yunus and 24 others from leaving the country pending completion of probe into the shortage of measles vaccines during his tenure . he is accused of having a severe shortage of vaccines between 2024 and 2025 .

title: Kerala: CM-elect VD Satheesan, 20 ministers to take oath today, portfolio talks on
content: Rajiv G is a senior assistant editor with the Times of India . he began his journey in 1998 with the Indian Express in Coimbatore before moving on to Deccan Chronicle in Hyderabad . since joining the Times in 2011, he has been at the forefront of reporting on the health sector .
